'''Rampa III''', commonly known as '''Rampa''', was a heavily industrialized [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]], which had a landscape so barren and polluted that drinkable [[Water/Legends|water]] was an extremely precious resource. The need for water set up a thriving [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] route known as the [[Rampa Rapids]] between Rampa III and nearby [[R'alla]], which had an abundance of clear, refreshing water. 

==Description==
Located in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]], Rampa III was located a short jump off the [[Solenbaran Merchant Route]], connected to [[R'alla]] by a [[Hyperspace route/Legends|route]] called the [[Rampa Rapids]]. By the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Rampa III was arid and polluted, and was constantly riddled by drought.<ref name="TEA" />

==History==
As of the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]], Rampa III had become heavily industrialized, dotted with [[Corporate Sector Authority/Legends|Corporate Sector Authority]] labor camps and factories. The Authority made it a practice to send [[Prison/Legends|prisoners]] and the sector's least fortunate to Rampa III, and the planet's landscape had become extremely polluted, with very little drinkable [[Water/Legends|water]]. As such, a bustling [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] trade sprung up between Rampa III and nearby R'alla, which was well-known for its sweet, clear water. That run, known as the Rampa Rapids, was heavily patrolled by both Authority [[Gunship/Legends|gunships]]<ref name="TEA" /> and the native [[Rampa Skywatch]],<ref name="hsr">''[[Han Solo's Revenge]]''</ref> and was known for being highly perilous.<ref name="TEA" /> Many smugglers cut their [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] running water to Rampa III, including a young [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], who ran the Rampa Rapids during his early career.<ref name="hsc">''[[Han Solo and the Corporate Sector Sourcebook]]''</ref> Gunrunner [[Tiv Pocarno|Tiv "Poke" Pocarno]] was less successful than Solo, as the [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] of the ''[[Broken 'Spanner]]'', in a futile effort to pay back a sizable debt to [[Reuss VIII/Legends|Reuss VIII]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Torel Vorne]], botched a smuggling run to Rampa III and [[Death/Legends|died]] penniless.<ref name="stock">''[[Stock Ships]]''</ref>

[[C-3PX]] once completed a hit on Rampa III following a hit on [[Bonadan/Legends|Bonadan]].<ref name="TNEGtD">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Rampa was first mentioned in ''[[Han Solo's Revenge]]'', a novel written by [[Brian Daley]] and released in [[1979]].<ref name="hsr" /> 

The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' later identified the planet as Rampa III. Additionally, it created the [[Rampa Minor system/Legends|Rampa Minor system]] to differentiate [[Rampa 1]] and [[Rampa 2]] from Rampa III, establishing that they are not in the same [[Star system/Legends|system]].<ref name="TEA" />
